summary: A mixed‑quality audience recording of a low‑energy show, with occasional highlights like So Many Roads and Desolation Row
review: Most listeners find this Deer Creek performance flat and lacking energy, citing weak vocals and a short first set. A few songs—So Many Roads, Desolation Row, Deal, Althea, Ship of Fools, The Other One and Brokedown Palace—stand out for some fans, and the mix is described as crisp by a minority, while others complain of hiss and poor audio quality.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (1‑2 stars) criticize the show’s energy and performance, but several reviewers note strong moments in specific songs and occasional good mix quality, leading to a low‑mid overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Comments are split: some praise a crisp mix and clear Vince keyboard presence, while others report heavy hiss and overall bad quality, indicating an uneven audience recording.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a very short first set (only six songs) and a second set with lackluster song selection, contributing to the overall feeling of low energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed feedback – vocals described as weak by some, but his solo on Desolation Row praised by others.
band_member_comments.Keys: Positive remarks about Vince's groove and moments of a crisp keyboard mix.
